title: Chapter Eighty-Three: Battle for Possession (Part Two)
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Ma Jin led him to a sealed stone chamber in the small practice ground. The scar on his face twitched -- worry, poorly hidden beneath the bearing of a black-ranked master.

"Your new palm technique. The black ranks who saw your match that day would have grasped the pros and cons with one move. The technique isn't precise. Is that right?"

"That's right."

"Your ready admission is good. What is the foundation of your martial arts?"

"Jeong family dynamic technique."

"Right. Little Divine Monk Gak Jeong was compared to the Muscle Sutra itself. Your meridians' endurance is beyond what you imagine. You can be bold in experimenting -- unlike ordinary internal masters. Your whole body is firmly woven. You needn't greatly worry about qi deviation."

"The point is," Ma Jin said, and his tone was his uncle's now, not his commander's, "don't worry about energy circulation in the new palm technique. Circulate true qi unhesitatingly to refine precision. Palm techniques will be more useful than cultivation techniques for now."

"Thank you for the advice. It's time I should go."

"The Namgung direct bloodline's swords are frightening. Come back after beheading them. Without carelessness."

The scar twitched again. Yeon-shin bowed once and turned around.

*Desolate Sword. Northern Light. Thousand Silkworm Thread martial clothes. Money pouch. Travel pack. And martial arts.*

All newly equipped. Preparations complete.

---

The path leaving the main gate.

Yeon-shin and Namgung Hwa-sin walked the official road, each leading a horse. Two blue-ranked masters, conspicuous on the open road. No elders beside them. No escort. No Divine Sword Team.

"Where did the two go, they say?"

"Just that they received missions to pass through other regions first..."

*Until joining up, two rising talents travel alone?*

They mounted their horses. Spurred the steeds and rode hard, the gale beating at their faces, the early summer sun hammering down in waves of heat.

Yeon-shin felt it after half an hour.

A stream of wind carrying bright red colour, caressing his cheek. Energy waves from far ahead -- very familiar. Strangely refined amid irregularity.

*Weaker than usual. The energy waves are shallow.*

"Young Hero Namgung."

"You mean the chilling energy? I felt it."

"Blood art true qi."

"Doesn't seem Blood Swordmaster level."

"It's an apostle."

"Blood Flame Sect's Apostle?"

"Yes. Should be the Seventh."

"I've heard well. That she lived with Young Hero."

"You knew how to joke too?"

"I'm human too. More than that -- the energy waves are strange, for an Apostle's power..."

"If weakened it's an opportunity. If it was her original might, then it's already too late."

They leaped from their horses simultaneously. Yeon-shin's field of view suddenly widened.

She emerged from the treeline.

Flowing red martial robe. Black-lacquer hair. A single red eye opposite a black eyepatch. Blood at the corners of her mouth -- dark, unfamiliar against her porcelain skin. She seemed unable to recover from internal injuries. And yet --

*The severely weakened appearance is exaggerated pretence. She is still powerful.*

He raised a storm of movement technique with Hwa-sin and stopped exactly ten steps away.

"The headquarters must have fallen."

"What do you want? Coming all the way to Xiangyang."

"...You weren't home." She had been wearing a hazy smile since facing him. Even appearing pitiful at a glance. "Came to find you."

"What's with that state?"

"Are you worried?" Her smile brightened. "The process was a bit... rough. Your black ranks."

"Found you, so it's fine. Come here."

She staggered toward him. Internal injuries deep. He raised his hand to stop Hwa-sin's intervention. Fortunately, the White Qilin trusted him enough to halt.

*Strange.*

He observed her expression and energy waves until her thin breath reached his nape.

She embraced him. Carefully, like someone holding something that might break.

"Go back and make divine techniques for me. My young Grand Teacher. My little Sect Leader."

Her whisper tickled his ear.

*If it works on this one, it should work the same on Namgung family's direct line.*

"Your young Grand Teacher," he said calmly, "is no more."

Along with mantra operation, ominous true qi rose from his right hand. Dust wind shimmering golden under sunlight. The explosion roared.

---
